vulnerabilities.description: Cryptographic hash algorithms such as SHA-1 and MD5 are not suitable for use in modern applications. They are vulnerable to collision attacks and should not be used for secure hashing.

vulnerabilities.name: Serializable classes should have a serialVersionUID
vulnerabilities.description: Classes that implement Serializable should declare a serialVersionUID field to ensure version compatibility during deserialization.

vulnerabilities.name: Serialization methods should be used correctly
vulnerabilities.description: Custom serialization methods (writeObject, readObject) should be declared correctly with the proper signature to be effective.

vulnerabilities.name: Properties should not be used with [Obsolete]

vulnerabilities.name: Hardcoded API key or secret

vulnerabilities.name: Weak cryptographic algorithm MD5
vulnerabilities.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken; use SHA-256 or stronger

vulnerabilities.name: Weak cryptographic algorithm SHA1
vulnerabilities.description: SHA1 is deprecated for security; use SHA-256 or stronger

vulnerabilities.name: Weak cryptographic algorithm DES
vulnerabilities.description: DES is insecure; use AES with 256-bit keys

vulnerabilities.name: Weak cryptographic algorithm RC2
vulnerabilities.description: RC2 is insecure; use AES with 256-bit keys

vulnerabilities.name: ECB mode encryption

vulnerabilities.name: CORS wildcard origin

vulnerabilities.name: Missing anti-forgery token validation

vulnerabilities.name: Disabled SSL certificate validation

vulnerabilities.name: Regex denial of service (ReDoS)

vulnerabilities.name: Unvalidated redirect URL

bugs.name: End switch cases with break
bugs.description: Switch cases should end with an unconditional break statement to prevent fall-through to the next case.

bugs.name: Collection elements should not be replaced unconditionally
bugs.description: Avoid replacing all elements in a collection unconditionally within a loop, as this defeats the purpose of iteration.

bugs.name: IDisposable should be implemented correctly
bugs.description: Classes implementing IDisposable should follow the proper dispose pattern to ensure resources are released correctly.

bugs.name: The 'required' modifier should be used correctly
bugs.description: The 'required' modifier should be used appropriately in property declarations.

bugs.name: ArgumentException should be thrown with invalid arguments
bugs.description: When throwing ArgumentException or its subclasses, the parameterName should be provided to identify the invalid argument.

bugs.name: IEnumerable<T> should be implemented correctly
bugs.description: Classes implementing IEnumerable<T> should provide a proper implementation of the GetEnumerator method.

bugs.name: Short-circuit logic should be used in boolean contexts
bugs.description: Use && and || operators instead of & and | in boolean contexts to take advantage of short-circuit evaluation.

bugs.name: Reading Standard Output and Standard Error streams should not be done at the same time
bugs.description: Reading from both StandardOutput and StandardError streams simultaneously can cause deadlock in process execution.

bugs.name: IComparable.CompareTo should return zero when objects are equal
bugs.description: When implementing IComparable.CompareTo, objects that are considered equal should return 0.

bugs.name: Direct indexing is faster than LINQ methods on arrays
bugs.description: Using direct array indexing is more efficient than using LINQ methods like ElementAt on arrays.

bugs.name: Classes should not be too deep in inheritance tree
bugs.description: Avoid deep inheritance hierarchies as they make code harder to understand and maintain.

bugs.name: Values should not be modified during enumeration
bugs.description: Modifying a collection while enumerating it can cause runtime exceptions and unpredictable behavior.

bugs.name: Classes with 'IDisposable' members should implement 'IDisposable'
bugs.description: If a class has fields that implement IDisposable, the class itself should implement IDisposable to properly dispose of those resources.

bugs.name: Blocking calls in async methods should be avoided
bugs.description: Avoid using blocking calls like .Result or .Wait() in async methods as they can cause deadlocks.

bugs.name: Serialization methods should be used correctly
bugs.description: Custom serialization methods should follow the correct signature and pattern.

bugs.name: Non-serializable classes should not be serialized
bugs.description: Classes that are not marked as [Serializable] should not be used in serialization contexts.

bugs.name: Dummy arguments should not be used in indexers
bugs.description: Indexers should not contain dummy arguments that are not used in the implementation.

bugs.name: Type names should not match namespace names
bugs.description: Type names should not be the same as their containing namespace names to avoid confusion.

bugs.name: Resources should be disposed with 'using' statement
bugs.description: Disposable resources should be properly disposed using the 'using' statement or try-finally blocks.

bugs.name: 'is' should not be used with incompatible types
bugs.description: The 'is' operator should not be used to check types that are incompatible with the expression being checked.

bugs.name: 'ToString()' should not be called on strings
bugs.description: Calling ToString() on a string is redundant and should be avoided.

bugs.name: Type parameters should be constrained
bugs.description: Generic type parameters should have appropriate constraints to ensure type safety.

bugs.name: 'catch' blocks should not be empty
bugs.description: Empty catch blocks silently swallow exceptions and make debugging difficult.

bugs.name: 'async' methods should not return void
bugs.description: Async methods should return Task or Task<T> instead of void to enable proper exception handling and composability.

bugs.name: 'GC.KeepAlive' should not be used in finalizers
bugs.description: Using GC.KeepAlive in finalizers is unnecessary and can lead to unexpected behavior.

bugs.name: Null reference in LINQ FirstOrDefault without null check
bugs.description: FirstOrDefault may return null; check before accessing members

bugs.name: Async method without await
bugs.description: Async methods should await at least one operation

bugs.name: Async void method

bugs.name: Task.Wait blocking in async context

bugs.name: StringBuilder used for single concatenation

codeSmells.description: Interface names should follow the convention of starting with a capital 'I' followed by PascalCase.

codeSmells.name: Loops should not contain more than a single break or continue statement
codeSmells.description: Limit the number of break and continue statements in loops to improve readability and maintainability.

codeSmells.name: Generic type parameters should be named with 'T' prefix
codeSmells.description: Generic type parameters should follow the naming convention of starting with 'T' followed by a descriptive name.

codeSmells.name: Public static readonly fields should be constant
codeSmells.description: Fields that are public, static, and readonly should be declared as const instead if their value is known at compile time.

codeSmells.name: Local variables should not shadow class fields
codeSmells.description: Avoid using local variable names that are the same as class fields to prevent confusion.

codeSmells.name: Namespaces should follow the company namespace convention
codeSmells.description: Namespace declarations should follow the standard company naming convention.

codeSmells.name: Lambdas should be replaced with method groups
codeSmells.description: Simple lambdas that just call a method can be replaced with method group syntax for cleaner code.

codeSmells.name: Anonymous methods should not be used as event handlers
codeSmells.description: Avoid using anonymous methods as event handlers as they can cause memory leaks and make code harder to maintain.

codeSmells.name: Obsolete attributes should be removed
codeSmells.description: Code marked with [Obsolete] should be updated or removed to avoid using deprecated functionality.

codeSmells.name: Files should not contain multiple types
codeSmells.description: Each file should contain only one top-level type to improve code organization and maintainability.

codeSmells.name: Serializable classes should have a serialVersionUID
codeSmells.description: Classes implementing Serializable should declare a serialVersionUID field.

codeSmells.name: 'Params' should be used for variadic methods
codeSmells.description: Methods that accept variable numbers of arguments should use the params keyword.

codeSmells.name: Destructors should not be used
codeSmells.description: Avoid using destructors (finalizers) as they are non-deterministic and can cause performance issues.

codeSmells.name: Properties should not be used with [Obsolete]
codeSmells.description: Properties marked with [Obsolete] attribute indicate deprecated functionality that should be avoided.

codeSmells.name: Constructors should not be used to initialize static fields
codeSmells.description: Static fields should be initialized in static constructors or field initializers, not instance constructors.

codeSmells.name: Method too long (>50 lines)
codeSmells.description: Methods should be concise; extract sub-methods for readability

codeSmells.name: Class too large (>500 lines)
codeSmells.description: Large classes should be split following single responsibility principle

codeSmells.name: Too many parameters in method (>5)
codeSmells.description: Methods with many parameters should use parameter objects

codeSmells.name: Too many dependencies injected (>7)
codeSmells.description: Classes with many dependencies violate single responsibility

codeSmells.name: Nested ternary expressions
codeSmells.description: Nested ternary expressions reduce readability; use if-else

codeSmells.name: Magic number in code
codeSmells.description: Replace magic numbers with named constants

codeSmells.name: Magic string in code
codeSmells.description: Replace magic strings with named constants or enums

codeSmells.name: Empty method body
codeSmells.description: Empty methods should be removed or documented with a comment

codeSmells.name: TODO comment left in code
codeSmells.description: Resolve TODO comments before production release

codeSmells.name: Commented-out code
codeSmells.description: Remove commented-out code; use version control instead

codeSmells.name: Console.WriteLine in production code
codeSmells.description: Use proper logging framework instead of Console.WriteLine

codeSmells.name: Region directive overuse
codeSmells.description: Excessive #region usage may indicate class is too large

codeSmells.name: Partial class without justification
codeSmells.description: Partial classes should only be used for generated code

codeSmells.name: Public field instead of property
codeSmells.description: Use properties instead of public fields for encapsulation

codeSmells.name: Mutable public property on immutable type
codeSmells.description: Immutable types should use init-only or readonly properties

codeSmells.name: var not used for obvious types
codeSmells.description: Use var when the type is obvious from the right side

codeSmells.name: Explicit type when var is clearer
codeSmells.description: Use var for complex generic types to improve readability

codeSmells.name: Unnecessary null check on non-nullable type
codeSmells.description: Null checks on non-nullable types are unnecessary

codeSmells.name: Pattern matching not used where applicable
codeSmells.description: Use C# pattern matching for cleaner type checks

codeSmells.name: Switch expression not used where applicable
codeSmells.description: Use switch expressions for concise mapping logic

codeSmells.name: String concatenation in loop
codeSmells.description: Use StringBuilder for string concatenation in loops

codeSmells.name: LINQ used where simple loop is clearer
codeSmells.description: Prefer simple loops over complex LINQ chains

codeSmells.name: Excessive method chaining
codeSmells.description: Break long method chains into intermediate variables for readability

codeSmells.name: God class anti-pattern
codeSmells.description: Classes should have a single, well-defined responsibility

codeSmells.name: Service locator anti-pattern
codeSmells.description: Use constructor injection instead of service locator pattern

codeSmells.name: Static helper class overuse
codeSmells.description: Consider instance-based classes with DI instead of static helpers

codeSmells.name: Repository returning IQueryable
codeSmells.description: Repositories should return materialized results, not IQueryable

codeSmells.name: Business logic in controller
codeSmells.description: Controllers should delegate business logic to service layer

codeSmells.name: Business logic in entity
codeSmells.description: Keep business logic in services, not domain entities (anemic vs rich)

codeSmells.name: Missing interface for testability
codeSmells.description: Classes with external dependencies should implement interfaces

codeSmells.name: Async suffix missing on async methods
codeSmells.description: Async methods should have Async suffix by convention

codeSmells.name: Boolean parameter (flag argument)
codeSmells.description: Replace boolean parameters with enum or separate methods

codeSmells.name: Excessive comments explaining obvious code
codeSmells.description: Code should be self-documenting; remove obvious comments

codeSmells.name: Abbreviations in identifiers
codeSmells.description: Use full words in identifiers for clarity

codeSmells.name: Missing XML documentation on public API
codeSmells.description: Public APIs should have XML documentation comments

codeSmells.name: Obsolete attribute without message
codeSmells.description: [Obsolete] should include a message explaining the alternative

codeSmells.name: Premature optimization
codeSmells.description: Optimize only after profiling identifies bottlenecks

codeSmells.name: Deep inheritance hierarchy (>3 levels)
codeSmells.description: Prefer composition over deep inheritance hierarchies

codeSmells.name: Multiple classes in single file
codeSmells.description: Each class should be in its own file

codeSmells.name: File name does not match class name
codeSmells.description: File name should match the primary class name

codeSmells.name: Namespace does not match folder structure
codeSmells.description: Namespaces should match the folder structure

codeSmells.name: Missing sealed keyword on non-inheritable class
codeSmells.description: Seal classes not designed for inheritance

codeSmells.name: ConfigureAwait(false) inconsistency
codeSmells.description: Use ConfigureAwait consistently throughout the method
